codeReview.summary: The worker successfully created 8 mobile design-system primitives (BottomSheet, ActionSheet, BottomNav, MobileAppShell, FAB, SwipeableCard, PullToRefresh, ResponsiveContainer/Grid/Stack) adapted from the sibling ComponentShowcase. All components follow the project conventions: simple wording, no emojis, restrained icon usage, and proper CSS variable usage for theming. The implementation includes responsive dual-mode rendering (desktop modal + mobile sheet patterns), touch gesture support, and proper z-index integration with existing CSS variables. 37 comprehensive tests were added covering open/close lifecycle, keyboard accessibility, callbacks, and responsive class composition. All validators pass (1096 tests, lint, typecheck, build).

issues.description: eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps comment suppresses the ESLint warning for the handleClose dependency in useEffect. While the current implementation is intentional (handleClose should not change on every onClose change to avoid re-binding), this pattern could be improved by using useCallback for handleClose outside the component or by documenting why the dependency is intentionally omitted.

The handleClose callback uses a 250ms setTimeout for the close animation. This magic number is not documented and could be extracted to a constant for maintainability, though it matches the animation duration in global.css.

The slideInLeft animation referenced for the sidebar overlay is not defined in the added global.css keyframes. This will fall back silently but the animation may not play as expected. Should add @keyframes slideInLeft to global.css or use an existing animation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that BottomSheet and ActionSheet need to render both desktop and mobile variants simultaneously (hidden via CSS) rather than conditionally rendering based on viewport. This pattern allows for smoother responsive transitions and is worth documenting for future mobile primitive work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: BottomSheet.tsx:87-126 and ActionSheet.tsx:85-140 show dual rendering with 'hidden sm:flex' and 'sm:hidden' classes for desktop and mobile sections respectively. Test file notes this pattern: 'BottomSheet renders two sections (desktop modal + mobile sheet) simultaneously'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker consistently used env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for mobile components that need to account for iOS safe areas. This is a good pattern that should be documented in AGENTS.md or library files for future mobile work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: BottomSheet.tsx:104, BottomNav.tsx:30, FAB.tsx:68-69, MobileAppShell.tsx:55 all use pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)] or bottom-[calc(...+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))].

sharedStateObservations.observation: The animation keyframe naming convention uses simple descriptive names (fadeOut, slideDown, sheetSlideUp, sheetSlideDown) rather than prefixed names like other existing animations (o-shake, o-skeleton-pulse). This inconsistency should be noted for future animation additions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: global.css lines 189-210 show the new keyframes using unprefixed names, while lines 175-183 and 219-227 show existing prefixed animations (o-check-draw, o-shake, o-glass-in).

summary: PASS. The mobile design-system foundation feature delivers exactly what was requested: 8 well-implemented mobile primitives adapted from the sibling ComponentShowcase, with proper responsive behavior, touch support, accessibility (Escape key dismissal), and comprehensive test coverage. The implementation follows all mission guidance (simple wording, no emojis, restrained icons) and passes all validators. Three minor non-blocking issues were noted: an eslint-disable comment that could be refactored, an undocumented animation duration constant, and a missing slideInLeft keyframe definition. Three shared-state observations were captured: the dual-render pattern for responsive overlays, the safe-area-inset convention, and the animation naming inconsistency.
